name: Marji's Fudgies
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Marji Holt
Set oven to 325°F. Blend together well the first eight ingredients. Add flour and blend together. The dough will be very stiff. Burned out a mixer on a double batch; a bread mixer would do nicely, otherwise look for strong arms. Drop spoonfuls onto ungreased foil on a cookie sheet. Using foil allows you to remove one sheet and bake the next without moving the cookies. Bake for 12 minutes at 325°F. Ovens vary.
The first time making these, let the first batch cool enough to peel intact from the foil, taste, and determine if you prefer them that way. Some people like them cooked a bit longer and crunchy all the way through. Most prefer a crispy exterior with a chewy middle. Find the right baking time and temperature for your preference. If using small eggs, consider three instead.
